headline: Wildlife crossings reconnect habitats and protect endangered animals
meta_description: Growing adoption of wildlife crossings worldwide helps animals navigate fragmented habitats while reducing collisions and boosting genetic diversity.
summary_short: Wildlife crossings are spreading globally, helping animals cross roads safely and restoring genetic diversity in threatened populations.
body:
Roads have carved animal habitats into isolated fragments, leaving wildlife trapped and populations struggling. Wildlife crossings—bridges and underpasses designed for animals to safely cross roads—are changing that picture across the world. These structures help reconnect broken habitats, reduce the devastating collisions that kill millions of animals annually, and allow populations to mix and maintain genetic health.

What started as a novel experiment has grown into a widely adopted tool. From mountain passes to wetlands, communities and conservation groups are building these crossings and watching animals use them. The approach offers a concrete way to let nature flow again across landscapes fractured by human infrastructure.
